title: Harpax

The '''''Harpax''''' was an [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] that served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was part of the task force that was dispatched to destroy the forces formerly under the command of the defector [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Harkov]]. It trapped his [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]], [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']] while it took on supplies.

The ''Harpax'' used its [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] to trap the ''Protector'' and prevent it from entering hyperspace, but the ''Protector'' called in reinforcements. Harkov's [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] ''[[Akaga]]'' and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a light cruiser]] [[Warhawk (MC40a light cruiser)|''Warhawk'']] entered to aid the ship. ''Harpax'' was outnumbered and outmaneuvered by enemy ships.

The interdictor cruiser then called in reinforcements of its own, which arrived in the form of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Garrett (Star Destroyer)|''Garrett'']] with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] on board. All three Alliance warships were eliminated but the ''Harpax'' had sustained too much damage and was destroyed.

==Behind the scenes==
Although it is possible to save the ''Harpax'' in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], its destruction is confirmed in [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele's]] mission report in ''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]''. The cruiser is misspelled "''Harpox''" once in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] and three times on page XVII of the accompanying ''[[TIE Fighter Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', although it is correctly spelt on other pages.
